IsA: It is an icon of the Mother of God (Hodegetria).
HasA: It has a revered status in Orthodoxy.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its protection and defense of the western borders of Russia.
UsedFor: It is used for veneration in Orthodoxy.
It is used for protecting and defending the western borders of Russia.
Affects: It affects the western borders of Russia.
KnownFor: It is known for its revered status in Orthodoxy.
It is known for protecting and defending the western borders of Russia.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Byzantine Emperor Constantine IX Monomachos.
It is associated with Princess Anna.
It is associated with Prince Vsevolod Yaroslavich.
It is associated with Prince Vladimir II Monomakh.
It is associated with Metropolitan Jonah.
It is associated with Bishop Misael of Smolensk.
It is associated with Grand Duke Vasily the Dark.
It is associated with Ireneus (Falkovsky).
It is associated with the Iveron icon.
It is associated with the Vladimir icon.
OccurredIn: It occurred in 1046.
It occurred in 1095.
It occurred in 1101.
It occurred in 1239.
It occurred in 1456.
It occurred on January 18, 1456.
It occurred on July 28, 1456.
It occurred on August 5-6.
It occurred on the day of the Battle of Borodino.
It occurred in December 1941.
HeldIn: It was held in the Dormition Cathedral in Smolensk.
It was held in the Cathedral of the Annunciation.
It was held in the Savvino-Storozhevsky Monastery.
It was held in the Church of the Annunciation in Dorogomilovo.
It was held in the Smolensk Kremlin.
It was held in the church above the Dnieper Gate of the Smolensk Kremlin.
It was held in the Bogoroditse-Odigitrievsky Convent in the village of Bugabashevo.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Byzantine Emperor Constantine IX Monomachos.
Influenced: It influenced the continuity and dynastic closeness of Constantinople and Kievan Rus'.
